summary: PR 1358 attempted to match `HSD_HashSearch` in SysDolphin baselib by adding typed hash structs, forward declarations, and a decompiled implementation in `hash.c`. The PR was closed without merge and has no available review discussion, so its exact rejection reason is not evidenced in the slice.
what_changed: Added forward declarations for `HSD_Hash` and `HSD_HashEntry` to `src/sysdolphin/baselib/forward.h`.
Replaced the old `HSD_HashSearch(u32, const char* class_name, int)` declaration with a typed prototype: `HSD_ClassInfo* HSD_HashSearch(HSD_Hash*, void*, int*)`.
Added hash class wrapper types `HSD_HashClass` and `HSD_HashClassInfo`, including a `getidx` virtual/function pointer taking `HSD_Hash*`.

Implemented `HSD_HashSearch` in `hash.c`: obtains an index through `hash->parent.class_info->getidx(hash)`, asserts it is below `table_size`, calls `HashSearchEntry`, writes optional success status, returns `entry->value` cast to `HSD_ClassInfo*`, or `NULL`.
smart_moves: Modeled `HSD_HashSearch` around the object/class layout rather than keeping the previous untyped placeholder signature, which makes the call to `hash->parent.class_info->getidx(hash)` expressible in C.
Added forward declarations before using `HSD_Hash` and `HSD_HashEntry` across headers, avoiding circular include pressure.
Preserved the optional out-parameter behavior with `if (success != NULL)` and used `!!entry` to normalize the success value to 0 or 1.
Included a bounds assertion before indexing/searching, using `HSD_ASSERT(113, idx < hash->table_size)`, which is likely important for matching source line/assert metadata.
Kept `HashSearchEntry` as a separate declared helper rather than inlining speculative logic into `HSD_HashSearch`.
decomp_lessons: For baselib class-like objects, reconstruct enough of the parent/class-info structure to express virtual calls naturally; here `HSD_Hash` needs a parent whose `class_info` exposes `getidx`.
When matching small wrappers, the exact pointer types and out-parameter null checks can be decisive: `HSD_HashSearch` takes `HSD_Hash*`, `void* key`, and `int* success`.
Use forward declarations in `forward.h` when headers need opaque references to newly reconstructed baselib structs.
Do not leave legacy placeholder prototypes once the real object-oriented signature is known; stale prototypes can hide important ABI details.
Assertion line numbers such as `HSD_ASSERT(113, ...)` are evidence-bearing and should be preserved during matching attempts.
naming_conventions: Hash entry type is named `HSD_HashEntry`; the struct body is declared as `struct HSD_HashEntry`.
Hash object type is named `HSD_Hash`; the struct body is declared as `struct HSD_Hash`.
The PR used internal class wrapper names `_HSD_HashClass` and `_HSD_HashClassInfo` with public typedefs `HSD_HashClass` and `HSD_HashClassInfo`, mirroring existing baselib class naming patterns.
The helper is named `HashSearchEntry`, without an `HSD_` prefix in the diff.
The old parameter name `class_name` disappeared from the public prototype; the matched function treats the lookup key generically as `void* key`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Implement the function in the same high-level order shown by the diff: compute `idx`, assert `idx < table_size`, call `HashSearchEntry(hash, idx, key, NULL)`, conditionally write `*success`, then return cast `entry->value` or `NULL`.
Use `!!entry` for boolean normalization in the success out-parameter, which may help generate the expected compare/boolean code shape.
Cast `entry->value` to `HSD_ClassInfo*` at the return site rather than typing the hash value field more narrowly.
Keep `idx` and `entry` as local variables; the submitted code intentionally materializes both before later branches.
The exact include set for the implementation was `#include "hash.h"` followed by `#include "debug.h"`, needed for the struct definitions and `HSD_ASSERT`.
follow_up_queries: Check later PRs or commits that successfully matched `HSD_HashSearch` to see why this closed-unmerged attempt was superseded.
Search for current definitions of `HSD_Hash`, `HSD_HashEntry`, and `HashSearchEntry` to verify final accepted struct layout and prototypes.
Compare generated assembly for this implementation against the target to identify any mismatch not visible from the PR slice.
Look for review or Discord context around PR 1358 if the reason for closure matters, since no comments or reviews are present in this dump.
